mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: A rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending power, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates robust aggregate demand that outpaces supply constraints, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of GDPC1 and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a late-cycle expansion reflects supply-side bottlenecks and fading economic momentum, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases do not precede negative 20-day S&P 500 returns, with a hit rate below 40 percent over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 5.0 percent, restrictive monetary policy sharply curtails liquidity and equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where FEDFUNDS stays above 5.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a strengthening labor market that boosts corporate earnings expectations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE decline fails to generate a positive Rank IC greater than 0.03 for 20-day forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal renewed consumer confidence and construction sector activity, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases do not correlate with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns, showing a Rank IC below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in industrial production above 102.0 during a post-shock recovery phase indicates resolving supply chain constraints and renewed manufacturing output, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO crossing above 102.0 in recovery regimes fails to predict positive 5-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10.
